rationale: The citing paper is a marketing-focused tutorial on behavioral-economics models, explicitly including reference dependence/loss aversion, and the cited paper develops and validates context-dependent (including loss-aversion-based) models of the compromise effect. This is a strong overlap in constructs and modeling purpose, so the cited work could plausibly be a mechanism/theory or benchmark source rather than an incidental background citation. Even though the match evidence is only DOI-level with no in-text snippet, it is safer to keep for second-stage judgment than to drop.
